q: Several capacitors can be connected together to be used in a variety of applications.
There are two types of combinations of capacitors: parallel and series.
There are three types of combinations of capacitors: parallel, series, and perpendicular.
All capacitors connected in parallel have the same potential difference.
Capacitors connected in parallel always carry identical amounts of charges even if their capacitances differ.
If $Q$ is the total charge on three parallel capacitors, then $Q = Q_1 + Q_2 + Q_3$.
An equivalent capacitor has the same total charge $Q$ when the same potential difference $V$ is applied.
The equivalent capacitance of capacitors connected in parallel is the sum of the capacitances of the individual capacitors.
The equivalent capacitance $C_p$ of a parallel combination of $n$ identical capacitors is $C_p = nC$.
The equivalent capacitance $C_p$ of a parallel combination of $n$ identical capacitors is $C_p = \frac{C}{n}$.
When capacitors are connected in series, each capacitor has the same charge.
When capacitors are connected in series, each capacitor has the same potential difference.
Capacitors connected in series have different potential differences if their capacitances are different.
The total potential difference $V$ of a series combination of three capacitors is $V = V_1 + V_2 + V_3$.
When capacitors are connected in series, the reciprocal of the equivalent capacitance is the sum of the reciprocals of their individual capacitances.
The equivalent capacitance $C_s$ of a series combination of $n$ identical capacitors is $C_s = \frac{C}{n}$.
In a parallel connection of capacitors, each capacitor has the same charge.
In a series connection of capacitors, each capacitor has the same charge.
In a parallel connection of capacitors, the potential difference of each capacitor is the same.
A parallel-plate capacitor is the simplest capacitor.
A parallel-plate capacitor consists of two parallel metal plates separated by air or other insulating material.
The ratio of the capacitance with an insulating material to the capacitance with a vacuum is called the dielectric constant.
If one conductor of a capacitor has a charge $+Q$, the other has a charge $-Q$.
The charge of a capacitor is the sum of the magnitudes of the charges on both conductors combined.
The potential difference of a capacitor is twice the potential difference between the two conductors of the capacitor.
Relative permittivity is listed as one of the key words on page 144 of the textbook.
